title: CSAR
historical_anchors_in_scope: The Ho Chi Minh Trail interdiction air war over Laos (1969)
AC-119K gunship operations against Trail traffic
Combat Search and Rescue operations out of Nakhon Phanom (NKP), Thailand
HH-3E Jolly Green rescue helicopters and the Aerospace Rescue and Recovery Service
A-1 Skyraider 'Sandy' RESCAP escort and on-scene command
O-1 Bird Dog forward air control
Pathet Lao and PAVN (NVA) control of the Laotian panhandle
Soviet-supplied air defenses including the SA-2 surface-to-air missile
The officially deniable nature of the US air war in Laos (publicly acknowledged by Souvanna Phouma, June 1969)
US Type I cipher hardware (KL-7) and the intelligence stakes of its compromise
historical_anchors_out_of_scope: The 1975 fall of Saigon (belongs to 'The Beast Cometh')
The 1973 Paris Peace Accords and US withdrawal completion
The Siege of Dien Bien Phu (1954) on-page — permitted only as Coyle's backstory memory of McGoon, never dramatized in-scene
South Vietnam ground-combat operations (this is the Laos air war, not the in-country ground war)
voice_register: Elmore Leonard-style: lean, dialogue-forward, scenes start late and end early, spare description, strong individuated character voice, period-accurate detail through action not exposition.
operational_doctrine: CSAR mission: recover downed airmen alive (the PJ creed — that others may live).
Rescue package: Jolly Green (HH-3E), Sandy (A-1 Skyraider RESCAP, on-scene commander), Bird Dog FAC.
The Laos air war is officially deniable.
PJs ride the hoist/penetrator down; medics first, operators second.
Radio reception over karst and jungle is unreliable; transmitting can reveal position.
anti_patterns: AI-isms (it's not just X / a testament to / in a world where)
redundant character reintroductions after first appearance
lyrical or ornate metaphor (anti-Leonard); keep prose lean and concrete, no decorative flourishes
round-counting as an emphasized motif (de-emphasized per operator decision; keep only plot-necessary instances)
